extensionDescription.message: Format HTTP request, response, and WebSocket payloads directly inside Chrome DevTools.

appDescription.message: Format DevTools Network requests, responses, and WebSocket messages

requestFilterPlaceholder.message: Filter by URL, method, or status

emptyStateDescription.message: Open Network requests on the current page, and formatted request, response, and WebSocket data will appear here.

narrowViewportDescription.message: Please widen the DevTools window before using Request Formatter.

responseContentNotLoaded.message: Response content is not loaded yet. Select the Response tab to load it.

responseContentUnavailable.message: Response content is unavailable. Keep DevTools open and preserve the Network log when needed.

responseBase64Description.message: Chrome returned this response as base64. Decode it externally if it is binary data.

payloadPreviewOnlyTitle.message: [Preview only] Payload is too large to fully format in the panel ($count$ chars).

payloadPreviewOnlyDescription.message: Only the first $count$ chars are shown to keep the UI responsive.

websocketBinaryPayloadUnsupported.message: Binary frame ($size$ bytes). Raw payload decoding is not supported. You can still copy the raw payload.

websocketSummaryAll.message: $count$ messages. Keeping the latest $limit$ at most.

devtoolsUnavailable.message: The current page is not running in a Chrome DevTools Extension environment. Load the unpacked extension and open DevTools to use it.

websocketUnavailable.message: WebSocket message capture cannot be enabled in the current environment.

websocketEnabled.message: WebSocket message capture is enabled. Chrome may show a debugging notice at the top of the page.

websocketEnableFailed.message: Failed to enable WebSocket message capture: $error$
